mnemonics.title: CPU Components
mnemonics.content: CUA: CONTROL UNIT + ALU = THE HEART OF PROCESSING

mnemonics.title: Instruction Cycle Steps
mnemonics.content: F DES: FETCH -> DECODE -> EXECUTE -> STORE (REMEMBER FDES)

mnemonics.title: Bus Types
mnemonics.content: D A C: DATA bus carries data, ADDRESS bus carries addresses, CONTROL bus carries control signals (DAC = Direction And Control)

mnemonics.title: Von Neumann Architecture
mnemonics.content: S P M: Stored Program concept means both instructions and Data are stored in Memory (SPM)

acronyms.usage: Performs all arithmetic and logical operations in the CPU
Holds address of next instruction to be fetched
Holds memory address for read/write operations
Temporarily holds data being transferred to/from memory
Generates control signals to coordinate CPU operations
Stores intermediate results of arithmetic operations
Uses simple, fixed-length instructions with few addressing modes
Uses complex variable-length instructions with many addressing modes

visualTips.visualization: Draw CPU as a rectangle with ALU on one side, Control Unit on top, and Registers in the middle like small boxes. Show data flowing through buses connecting these components.
Use a circular diagram showing FETCH -> DECODE -> EXECUTE -> STORE with arrows forming a complete loop. Add decision points for branch instructions that can change the flow.
Think of a city road system: Address bus is like street signs (telling WHERE to go), Data bus is the road (carrying PEOPLE/data), Control bus is traffic signals (managing WHEN to go).
Draw an inverted triangle with Registers at top (smallest, fastest), then Cache, then Main Memory, then Secondary Storage at bottom (largest, slowest).

analogies.analogy: The CPU is like the brain of the computer - it processes information, makes decisions, and controls all other parts. The Control Unit is like the brain's processing center, and ALU is like the calculator.
A computer bus is like a highway: wider buses (more lanes) can carry more data at once, just like wider highways carry more cars. Speed is limited by the slowest component.

Registers are like a notepad on your desk - fast to access for quick notes. Main memory is like a filing cabinet - slower but holds more. Hard drive is like a warehouse - slowest but stores everything.
Executing an instruction is like following a recipe: FETCH reads the recipe (from memory), DECODE understands the steps, EXECUTE actually cooks, STORE saves the result.
